description_long: This is a medieval-style war hammer with a long, cylindrical wooden handle reinforced by metal bands and capped at both ends with metal. The head features a prominent, sharply pointed spike on one side and a flat, hammering face on the opposite, with two additional short spikes forming a cross pattern. The metal head and reinforcements have a dark, weathered finish, suggesting heavy use or age. The handle appears to be made of dark-stained wood, showing some texture and minor surface irregularities. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for both striking and piercing tasks in an urban or historical context.
description: A medieval-style war hammer with a long wooden handle, metal reinforcements, and a spiked, cross-shaped metal head.
description_view_0: The hammer is shown at an angle, highlighting the long wooden handle and the cross-shaped metal head with a prominent spike.
description_view_1: A direct view of the hammer's head, emphasizing the pointed spike and the symmetrical cross pattern of the metal.
description_view_2: The opposite angled view, showing the handle and the head's flat striking face along with the side spikes.
description_view_3: A side profile of the hammer, displaying the full length of the handle and the alignment of the head's spikes.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, dark-stained, reinforced with metal bands, smooth with minor texture.
attribute_head_type: Cross-peen hammer head with a long, pointed spike, flat striking face, and two short side spikes forming a cross.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Minor surface irregularities and weathering on metal, no major damage visible.
